rows.notes: Added from image, identified by gemini-flash. gpt-5-mini missed this header entirely.

rows.description: Soluta Varia.
rows.notes: Consensus across both extractors for description and type.

rows.description: To Mr Vincent. Deficiency of Salary to Nov. 2. 1820.
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash as it correctly spells 'Deficiency' (gpt-5-mini had 'Defiency'), confirmed by visual inspection.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Vincent for a deficiency (shortfall) in his salary up to 2 November 1820.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Vincent for a deficiency in his salary up to 2 November 1820, amounting to £60 10s 6d.

rows.description: To Rowell for the Clock, its fulling
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed by visual inspection. gpt-5-mini misread 'Rowell' as 'Crowell' and 'its fulling' as 'at Fulham'.
rows.english_description: Payment to Rowell for work on the college clock (repairing/fitting).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Rowell for work on the college clock, amounting to £31 0s 0d.

rows.description: To the College Servants, as occasional Proctor's
rows.notes: From gemini-flash. This is the first line of a two-line description, with no associated amount on this line, reflecting the ledger's physical layout. gpt-5-mini combined this with the next line and placed the amount there.
rows.english_description: Payment to college servants for acting as occasional proctors (on the nights of the Illumination and similar events).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to college servants for acting as occasional proctors on the nights of the Illumination and similar events, amounting to £1 10s 0d.

rows.description: men on the nights of the Illumination &c.
rows.notes: From gemini-flash. This is the second line of a two-line description, carrying the amount. Confirmed by visual inspection.
rows.english_description: Payment to college servants for acting as occasional proctors on the nights of the Illumination and similar events.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to college servants for acting as occasional proctors on the nights of the Illumination and similar events, amounting to £1 10s 0d.

rows.description: To the Exec. of the late Rector D. Cole, as his
rows.notes: From gemini-flash. First line of a two-line description, no amount on this line. The name 'D. Cole' is confirmed visually. gpt-5-mini combined this line and misread 'Cole' as 'Cle'.
rows.english_description: Payment to the executors of the late Rector D. Cole for his share of the arrears of the Gosford tithes.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the executors of the late Rector D. Cole for his share of the arrears of the Gosford tithes, amounting to £122 12s 0d.

rows.description: share of the Arrears. of the Gosford Tithes
rows.notes: Amount from gemini-flash, confirmed visually (122 pounds). gpt-5-mini, in its combined row, read '120' pounds, which is incorrect. Description confirmed visually.
rows.english_description: Payment to the executors of the late Rector D. Cole for his share of the arrears of the Gosford tithes.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the executors of the late Rector D. Cole for his share of the arrears of the Gosford tithes, amounting to £122 12s 0d.

rows.description: To Munday & Slatter for Advertisements
rows.notes: Amount from gemini-flash, confirmed visually (1 pound, 7 shillings). gpt-5-mini misread the column alignment, interpreting '1 7' as '17' pounds. Description 'Advertisements' confirmed visually (gpt-5-mini had 'advertisement to').
rows.english_description: Payment to Munday & Slatter for placing advertisements.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Munday & Slatter for advertisements, amounting to £1 7s 0d.

rows.description: To Mr Shelres. his Bill
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. The surname is clearly 'Shelres' in the image (gpt-5-mini read 'Phelbe').
rows.english_description: Payment of Mr Shelres's bill (unspecified charges).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment of Mr Shelres's bill, amounting to £54 6s 6d.

rows.description: -- Leach's Bill. Carpenter, for 1820 --
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually for exact punctuation.
rows.english_description: Payment to Leach, carpenter, for work done in 1820.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Leach, carpenter, for work in 1820, amounting to £154 14s 7d.

rows.description: -- Knowles'. Mason -- Do --
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually for exact punctuation and casing of 'Do'.
rows.english_description: Payment to Knowles, mason, for work (account for 1820).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Knowles, mason, for work in 1820, amounting to £64 10s 5d.

rows.description: -- Hemming & Bowles. Plumbers &c. -- Do --
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. The surname is 'Bowles' in the image (gpt-5-mini read 'Bowler').
rows.english_description: Payment to Hemming & Bowles for plumbing and related work (account for 1820).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Hemming & Bowles for plumbing and related work in 1820, amounting to £85 6s 0d.

rows.description: -- Hewlett. Plaisterer -- Do --
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. The trade is 'Plaisterer' (an older spelling of 'plasterer') in the image (gpt-5-mini read 'Plaiteror').
rows.english_description: Payment to Hewlett, plasterer, for work (account for 1820).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Hewlett, plasterer, for work in 1820, amounting to £125 2s 5d.

rows.description: -- Harris & Plowman. Builders. on acct --
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually for exact punctuation ('--' at end).
rows.english_description: Payment to Harris & Plowman, builders, on account of building works.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Harris & Plowman, builders, on account, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.description: Domus Wine Battels. Mids. & Mich. 1820
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. The word is 'Battels' (gpt-5-mini read 'Bottles') and 'Mids.' (gpt-5-mini read 'Mid.').
rows.english_description: Wine battels (house wine charges) for the college at Midsummer and Michaelmas 1820.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Wine battels for the college at Midsummer and Michaelmas 1820, amounting to £6 15s 6d.

rows.description: Feb. 5 To Stephen Davis for Water-pipes
rows.notes: Amount from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. gpt-5-mini misread shillings '8' as '5'. Description 'Water-pipes' confirmed visually (gpt-5-mini omitted hyphen).
rows.english_description: Payment to Stephen Davis for supplying or fitting water-pipes.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Stephen Davis for water-pipes, amounting to £2 8s 0d.

rows.description: 27. A Stamp receipt to Mr Dallaway
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually for '27.' and 'A' (gpt-5-mini had '— a').
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Dallaway for a stamp receipt (stamp duty charge).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Dallaway for a stamp receipt, amounting to £0 5s 0d.

rows.description: Mar. 28. Interest to Library Fund. 2 Days. 1821
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. Date is '28.' and '2 Days' in the image (gpt-5-mini had '25.' and '1 Day').
rows.english_description: Interest paid to the Library Fund for two days in 1821.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Interest paid to the Library Fund for two days in 1821, amounting to £21 12s 0d.

rows.description: Apr. 12. To John Warren as watchman for 3 weeks
rows.notes: Amount from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. gpt-5-mini misread the pounds '1' as blank and shillings '5' as '15'.
rows.english_description: Payment to John Warren for services as watchman for three weeks.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to John Warren as watchman for three weeks, amounting to £1 5s 0d.

rows.description: May. 1. Domus Wine Battels. 2 Day. 1821
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. The word is 'Battels' (gpt-5-mini read 'Bottles') and '2 Day' (gpt-5-mini read 'Day').
rows.english_description: Wine battels for the college for two days in 1821.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Wine battels for the college for two days in 1821, amounting to £4 5s 0d.

rows.description: May. 7. To Mr Tubb. Attorney's Charges for had Lane leases.
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. The name is 'Tubb' (gpt-5-mini read 'Tabb') and the phrase is 'had Lane leases' (gpt-5-mini read 'distance leases').
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Tubb for attorney's charges in connection with the Had Lane leases.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Tubb for attorney's charges for the Had Lane leases, amounting to £1 6s 6d.

rows.description: 20. Subscription to Carfax Church.
rows.notes: Amount from gemini-flash, confirmed visually (3 pounds). gpt-5-mini misread the '3' as '30' pounds. Description confirmed visually.
rows.english_description: Subscription (contribution) to Carfax Church.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Subscription to Carfax Church, amounting to £3 0s 0d.

rows.description: 16. To Messr. Felkin H.P. postage on purchase of Stock
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. The name is clearly 'Felkin H.P.' in the image (gpt-5-mini read 'Gilbert &c.').
rows.english_description: Payment to Messrs. Felkin for postage on the purchase of stock (investment transaction expense).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Messrs. Felkin for postage on purchase of stock, amounting to £0 2s 8d.

rows.description: Nov. 2. 1820. To Mr Forbes, at Cocks & Biddulphs
rows.notes: From gemini-flash. First line of a two-line description, no amount on this line. The name 'Biddulphs' is confirmed visually (gpt-5-mini combined this line and misread 'Biddulphs' as 'Riddulphs').
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Forbes, at Cocks & Biddulphs, on account of his legal work for the Gosford cause (second bill).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Forbes at Cocks & Biddulphs for his second bill on account of the Gosford cause, amounting to £88 6s 2d.

rows.description: his second Bill, on acct. of the Gosford Cause --
rows.notes: From gemini-flash. Second line of a two-line description with the associated amount. Amount confirmed visually.
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Forbes, at Cocks & Biddulphs, on account of his legal work for the Gosford cause (second bill).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Forbes at Cocks & Biddulphs for his second bill on account of the Gosford cause, amounting to £88 6s 2d.

rows.description: June 9 Receipt Stamp. for Yarnton Tithes
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually for 'Stamp.' and 'Tithes' (gpt-5-mini had 'Stamp' and 'Tythes').
rows.english_description: Payment for a receipt stamp relating to the Yarnton tithes.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for a receipt stamp for the Yarnton tithes, amounting to £0 2s 6d.

rows.description: 25. To Mr Dalby. Expences of the Progreys.
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. 'Expences' is the correct historical spelling (gpt-5-mini had 'Expenses'), and 'Progreys' is a more accurate transcription of the script than 'Progress' (gpt-5-mini).
rows.english_description: Expenses paid to Mr Dalby for the Progress (the Rector's estate inspection tour).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Expenses paid to Mr Dalby for the Progress (estate inspection), amounting to £22 4s 9d.

rows.description: 28. To a special Messenger to little Tew with letters to
rows.notes: From gemini-flash. First line of a two-line description, no amount on this line. The date is '28.' and place name 'Tew' confirmed visually (gpt-5-mini combined this line and misread the date as '30.' and place as 'Bus').
rows.english_description: Payment to a special messenger sent to Little Tew to deliver letters to the tenants regarding their fines.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to a special messenger to Little Tew to deliver letters to the tenants about their fines, amounting to £1 1s 0d.

rows.description: the Tenants respecting their Fines
rows.notes: From gemini-flash. Second line of a two-line description with the associated amount. Amount confirmed visually.
rows.english_description: Payment to a special messenger sent to Little Tew to deliver letters to the tenants regarding their fines.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to a special messenger to Little Tew to deliver letters to the tenants about their fines, amounting to £1 1s 0d.

rows.description: June. 11. Receipt Stamps for Merton Precent -
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. The word is clearly 'Precent' (gpt-5-mini read 'Rent').
rows.english_description: Payment for receipt stamps for the Merton precentor.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment for receipt stamps for the Merton precentor, amounting to £0 2s 6d.

rows.description: July 6 To Mr Johnson. Dividends. p. 418-419. v. comp. 1820.
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors, including the fraction. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually. Date is '6' and page numbers '418-419' (gpt-5-mini had '8' and '416-419').
rows.english_description: Payment to Mr Johnson described as dividends (see ledger reference).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Mr Johnson described as dividends, amounting to £26 9s 10.75d.

rows.description: 10 To the Oxford Gas Co for the light -
rows.notes: Amounts are identical in both extractors. Description from gemini-flash, confirmed visually for exact punctuation ('-' at end) and 'Co'.
rows.english_description: Payment to the Oxford Gas Company for gas lighting.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Oxford Gas Company for gas lighting, amounting to £6 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Consensus across both extractors for the total amount and empty description. The fraction is clearly '¾' in the image.
